AI Runs in the Cloud — So Why Does Opening More Agents Lag Your Computer?

Claude's model lives in the cloud, so how can Claude Code read your project, edit files, and run commands? A beginner-friendly look at the remote-brain / local-hands split — Coding Agents, tool calls, GPU, memory, and why more Agents lag your machine.
New Claude Code Hijacks Mouse Clicks in Your Terminal? Kill It with CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE_CLICKS

When you're working in Claude Code and try to select text in the terminal or click a link in an error message, the clicks get hijacked by Claude Code itself. This post walks through the difference between CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE and CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MOUSE_CLICKS, how to add it to your .zshrc, and the one-off bypass hotkeys for iTerm2, macOS Terminal, and VS Code when you don't want to touch your config.
design.md: If Your AI-Generated UI Keeps Drifting, You're Missing Google's design.md

design.md is a format spec that Google Labs incubated from their AI design tool Stitch and then open-sourced. The goal is to make AI Coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot actually understand your design system. This post covers what design.md is, how it's different from rules files like CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md, what the mixed YAML Token + Markdown rationale structure looks like, a real example (Heritage), and what the built-in CLI can do (lint with WCAG contrast checks, diff, export to Tailwind v3/v4 or W3C DTCG, spec) — plus how to wire it into your AI Coding tool of choice.
How Much of Your Post Does AI Search Actually Grab? Breaking Down Claude's WebSearch and WebFetch

This post digs into how much of your blog AI actually pulls in when it does a web search. I went through Anthropic's official web_search and web_fetch tool specs, broke down the fact that the search stage only returns title + URL, the 150-character cited_text cap, and Claude Code's built-in WebFetch using Haiku 3.5 to summarize a 100 KB truncation, then ran a real test against my own blog. Wraps up with four actionable AEO writing tips: titles that carry their own context, lead with the answer, design single sentences that can be quoted standalone, and use structure as navigation markers.
Is Claude Code Auto Memory Hogging Your Memory? A Cleanup & Diagnostics Guide

Opened `~/.claude/projects/` and noticed it keeps growing? The first instinct is usually to blame Auto Memory. Turns out Auto Memory doesn't eat your RAM at all — it actually eats Context Window (MEMORY.md loads only the first 200 lines or 25KB) and disk space. And the real culprit bloating that folder isn't memory files, it's conversation jsonl. This post walks through what Auto Memory actually consumes, the `cleanupPeriodDays` auto-cleanup mechanism, `CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_PROMPT_HISTORY` for fully disabling transcript writes, and how to use `find` to clean out large files manually.
What Are Claude Code Hooks? Auto-Run Lint, Block rm -rf, Inject Context on SessionStart — A Complete Guide

When you're developing with Claude Code, do you find yourself constantly telling it to run Prettier again? Or worrying that one day it'll accidentally fire off rm -rf? This post walks through how Claude Code Hooks work — when events like PreToolUse, PostToolUse, UserPromptSubmit, SessionStart, Notification, and Stop fire, how to set up matcher and command in settings.json, how to block actions with exit code 2 — and runs through practical hooks: auto-format, blocking rm -rf, reinjecting project context after Compact, Mac desktop notifications, plus prompt / agent hook types and /hooks debugging.
Claude Code Routines: How to Set Up /schedule (and Why It Pushes to claude/ Instead of main)

I scheduled a task with Claude Code's /schedule, came back to the repo next day, and asked myself: I told it to push to main, why are there a bunch of claude/xxx branches instead? This post walks through Claude Code's three scheduling options (Routines, Desktop scheduled tasks, /loop), how /schedule works on the CLI, Routines' three trigger types (Schedule, API, GitHub event), custom cron rules, the 1-hour minimum interval — and most importantly, why pushes land on claude/-prefixed branches and how to flip "Allow unrestricted branch pushes" to fix that confusing behavior.
Vibe Coding's 2026 Upgrade: A Six-Stage Agentic Engineering SOP with Templates and a Real Case

I've written about what Vibe Coding is and the basics newcomers should pick up. This post is the 2026 upgrade — the full playbook. In April 2026 Karpathy himself, at the Sequoia Ascent talk, upgraded vibe coding into agentic engineering. So this post takes the agentic engineering discipline and rebuilds the AI development workflow from scratch — three-layer documentation (Spec / Plan / Tasks via CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md, ROADMAP, tasks.md templates), AI employee division of labor (Skill vs Sub-agent vs Agent Teams), development discipline (tests in the same turn, UI delegation, Conventional Commits), two-layer wrap-up validation (typecheck + test + build, plus Writer/Reviewer mode and AI citation cross-checks), a rollback decision table, and a two-tier Memory + CLAUDE.md architecture. The whole thing runs end-to-end against a personal e-commerce MVP (Nuxt 3 + Supabase + Stripe), including how to defuse the classic Stripe-checkout client-price-tampering security trap.
What Are Claude Code's Built-in Tools Doing? Understand How It Picks Tools — Then Write Better Prompts

This post is a quick chat about Claude Code's built-in Tools. If you've used Claude Code, you've probably noticed something — sometimes when you ask it to read a file, it just opens it with Read. Other times when you ask "what components does this project have," it goes off and runs Glob and Grep. And occasionally, it takes a detour through Bash's cat or grep, which is confusing because the built-in tools are right there. Claude actually has its own logic for picking tools — this post walks through what built-in Tools Claude Code has, how it decides which one to use, and how to reverse-engineer its tool usage to fix your prompts.
AI Agent Isn't One Thing Anymore — Understanding Coding Agents and Other AI Agent Types

Back when I wrote about AI terminology, AI Agent was used as a catch-all term for systems that can understand a task on their own, break it into steps, and use tools to get things done. But here in 2026, the term AI Agent has started to splinter into more specific subcategories — and the most mature one is the Coding Agent (some people call it Code Agent). This post walks through what these categories actually mean and how they differ.
